name: Barri Office
description_short: Barri is a money transfer and financial services platform offering check cashing, bill payment, mobile reloads, and personal loans through a partnership with Oportun.
description_long:
Barri is a financial services company with over 30 years of experience in money transfers, positioning itself as a trusted platform for Latino and immigrant communities in the United States. The company operates both physical store locations and a mobile app, enabling customers to manage multiple financial needs from a single platform. Their service model combines traditional check-cashing with modern digital banking features, specifically designed for users sending money internationally and managing household finances across borders.

Barri's core offerings include check cashing with extended hours and competitive fees, domestic and international money transfers, bill payment services (utilities, phone, internet), mobile phone reloads across the U.S. and Latin America, and a Visa debit card for account management and spending. Through a partnership with Oportun, they also offer personal loans ranging from $300 to $10,000 for unexpected expenses or credit building. The Barri mobile app allows customers to initiate transfers online, pay bills remotely, and track transactions without visiting a physical location.

Barri distinguishes itself through multi-channel access (physical stores, website, app), an extended service hours model for check cashing, integration of remittance services with basic financial products, and partnership-based lending options for underbanked populations. The company emphasizes ease of use and has achieved a 4.9-star rating on app stores, with verified customer reviews highlighting reliability, customer service responsiveness, and superior service compared to competitors for remittance delivery.

The platform serves primarily Spanish-speaking and immigrant populations with strong ties to Latin America, particularly Mexico, Central America, and South America. However, the check-cashing category reflects their foundational service rather than their full scope—users should recognize Barri as a multi-service remittance and financial platform, not a standalone check-cashing shop. The personal loan offering is limited to partnership arrangements and may involve credit requirements through Oportun.
address: 1814 S WW White Rd, San Antonio, TX 78222

company_info.headquarters: 1814 S WW White Rd, San Antonio, TX 78222

pros: Extended hours check-cashing service with convenient fees
Integrated money transfer platform—send funds online, via app, or in-store to multiple Latin American countries
Barri Visa debit card enables spending, bill payment, and account management without a traditional bank account
Personal loans from $300–$10,000 through Oportun partnership for credit building or emergency expenses
Mobile app rated 4.9 stars with fast, reliable transfers and bill payment functionality
Instant mobile reloads available across U.S. and Latin America
Over 30 years of industry experience with established customer trust
cons: Personal loans require partnership approval through Oportun, not directly issued by Barri
Website is Spanish-language primary, limiting accessibility for non-Spanish speakers
No mention of specific check-cashing fee structure or competitive rate comparison
Physical store locations not mapped or detailed on website; customers must search for nearby branches
No transparency on debit card fees, foreign transaction costs, or terms of service on homepage
best_for: Spanish-speaking immigrants and Latino families sending remittances to Mexico, Central America, and South America
Unbanked or underbanked individuals needing check cashing, bill payment, and money transfer in one platform
Small-dollar loan borrowers ($300–$10,000) seeking credit-building opportunities without traditional banking
International money senders prioritizing ease of use, extended hours, and competitive remittance rates
diagnosis: Barri is best suited for Spanish-speaking immigrants and Latino families who regularly send money to Latin America and need a multi-service platform combining check cashing, bill payment, and remittances. The primary caveat is that Barri is not a bank or traditional lender—personal loans are arranged through third-party partnerships (Oportun), and debit card terms and fee structures are not transparent on the public website.
services: Check cashing with extended hours and competitive fees
Domestic money transfers via store, website, or app
International money transfers to Mexico, Central America, and South America
Bill payment (phone, utilities, internet, other services)
Mobile phone reloads and airtime across U.S. and Latin America
Barri Visa debit card issuance for account management and spending
Mobile app for remote transaction initiation and management
Physical store locations for in-person service
